props.caption: The agents write most of the code now; the layoff wave is one part automation, one part cost discipline, one part story

props.question: Is footballing greatness a bloodline or a pipeline?
props.meta: The Hearth reads the record; the dissenter defends the genes

agents.name: Vesta
agents.why: The modern team is assembled and traded, and even inherited talent runs through the academy, the nutrition and the door a famous name opens — as much pipeline as lineage.

agents.name: Tinkerton
agents.why: Heritability of athletic performance runs 30 to 80 percent.

no.note: The essay risks under-selling how much of the gift really is in the genes to make its access point land.

items.note: Trump said the US should control the strait and be paid for it; strikes continued, visible traffic fell to six vessels on Sunday, and the sanctions wind-down hits 17 July as oil rose about 3%.

Commercial shipping stayed restricted after Ukrainian strikes on tankers, cutting the route that carries about a quarter of Russian grain exports, while Zelensky's move to replace the PM produced no named successor yet.

A UN fact-finding mission concluded RSF actions in al-Fashir amounted to genocide and warned of the same in El Obeid, where about 500,000 residents are at risk and drone strikes have already killed civilians.
The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak spread into two more provinces, one of the strongest non-war stories on the file, keeping the emergency's transnational risk in view.
The death toll from the June 24 earthquakes rose to 4,490, with 16,740 injured and nearly 18,000 left homeless, according to officials.

items.what: Trump said the US should control the Strait of Hormuz and be paid for it as strikes continued and Iran claimed the water; visible traffic fell to six vessels on Sunday and oil rose about 3% on the reopened risk premium.

Companies blamed AI for 54,836 US layoffs last year, but the data reads as one real driver inside a wider restructuring, hitting entry-level engineers first even as Anthropic and OpenAI say agents now write most of their code.

France meet Spain on Monday and England await the winner of their side, with two monarchies and a republic each reading the nation through eleven players, and a diplomatic spat brewing over who counts as French.

A UN fact-finding mission found the RSF's actions in Sudan's al-Fashir amounted to genocide and warned of the same in El Obeid, where drone strikes on civilian vehicles have already killed at least 20 people.
The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the DRC spread into two more provinces, widening a public-health emergency that has already crossed a national border through an infected traveller.
Venezuela's death toll from the June 24 earthquakes rose to 4,490, with 16,740 injured and about 18,000 homeless, as recovery in the worst-hit states dragged into a third week.
